title.en: Not a good time: "It's a bit difficult to talk right now."
title.ko: 지금은 통화하기가 좀 어려워서요 (갑작스러운 연락 거절하기)

description.en: Busy when someone calls or visits? Learn how to politely decline and postpone the conversation using the phrase "어려워서요."
description.ko: 예고 없이 걸려온 전화나 방문이 당황스러울 때! 무례하지 않게 상황을 설명하고 나중을 기약하는 정중한 선 긋기 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Phone. Visit. Boundary. Polite Refusal. Etiquette. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I love my friends, but sometimes they call me out of the blue when I'm right in the middle of a meeting.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I don't want to just ignore the call, but saying "I'm busy" feels a bit too blunt in Korean. Is there a softer way to tell them I can't talk right now?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "바빠요" 라고만 하면 상대방이 서운해할 수 있죠.
content.translation: Right. If you just say "I'm busy," the other person might feel hurt.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 때는..., "어려워서요" 라는 단어를 써보세요. 지금 내 상황이 대화를 이어가기 쉽지 않다는 것을 아주 부드럽게 전달해 주거든요.
content.translation: In those cases, try using the word "Eoryeowoseoyo." It conveys very softly that your current situation makes it not easy to continue the conversation.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "어려워서요"? I thought that only meant "difficult" as in a hard exam!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 한국어에서는 "안 된다" 혹은..., "불가능하다" 라는 말을 대신해서 정말 많이 쓰여요.
content.translation: Haha, in Korean, it's used a lot instead of saying "No" or "Impossible."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 갑작스러운 상황에서 정중하게 나만의 시간을 지키는 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn reactions to politely protect your own time in sudden situations!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 지금은 통화하기가 좀 어려워서요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: This sounds much less aggressive than "I can't talk now."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 내 의지보다 상황을 강조하는 표현이라서 그래요.
content.translation: Yes. That's because it's an expression that emphasizes the 'situation' rather than your will.

insight.title: ~하기가 어려워서요
insight.usage_tip: 통화하다(To talk on the phone) + 기가(Making a verb a noun) + 어려워서요(Because it is difficult). This is a standard polite way to decline a request or a situation.

content.speaker: John
content.text: And how do I promise to call back later?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "나중에 제가 다시 전화드릴게요" 라고 덧붙여 보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try adding "I'll call you back later."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 미안함과 정중함을 동시에 표현하는 완벽한 마무리 문장이에요.
content.translation: It's a perfect closing sentence that expresses both apology and politeness at the same time.

insight.title: 나중에 제가 다시 전화드릴게요
insight.usage_tip: 나중에(Later) + 제가(I/Polite) + 다시(Again) + 전화드릴게요(Will give a call/Honored). Using '드릴게요' instead of '할게요' adds a layer of respect.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm in a cafe finishing up some work, and Mina suddenly calls me to chat.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 지금 뭐 해요? 저 이번에 진짜 맛있는 맛집 찾아냈는데 같이 갈래요?
content.translation: John! What are you doing now? I found a really delicious restaurant this time, do you want to go together?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아! 미나! 맛집요? 정말 가고 싶네요.
content.translation: Ah, Mina! A restaurant? I'd really love to go.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그런데 어쩌죠? 제가 지금은 통화하기가 좀 어려워서요.
content.translation: But what should I do? It's a bit difficult for me to talk right now.

insight.title: 지금은 통화하기가
insight.usage_tip: Use this when you are in a quiet place or in the middle of a task.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아~! 바쁘시구나! 제가 눈치 없이 전화를 걸었네요. 미안해요!
content.translation: Ah, you're busy! I called without realizing the situation. Sorry!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니에요. 정리되는 대로 제가 다시 전화드릴게요.
content.translation: No, it's not. I'll call you back as soon as I'm done.

insight.title: 정리되는 대로
insight.usage_tip: This shows that you are willing to call back soon.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네, 알겠어요! 이따가 연락 줘요!
content.translation: Okay, I see! Call me later!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Phew! It was much easier to end the call when I used "어려워서요" instead of an excuse.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 자신의 상황을 정중하게 표현하면 상대방도 기분 나빠하지 않고 기다려 줄 수 있으니까요.
content.translation: Right. If you express your situation politely, the other person can wait without feeling bad.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "busy" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 지금은 통화하기가 좀 어려워서요!
content.translation: It's a bit difficult for me to talk right now!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 나중에 제가 다시 전화드릴게요!
content.translation: I'll call you back later!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 지금은 방문하기가 좀 곤란해서요!
content.translation: It's a bit awkward for you to visit right now!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 일이 끝나는 대로 바로 연락드릴게요!
content.translation: I'll contact you as soon as I finish work!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이해해 주셔서 정말 감사해요!
content.translation: Thank you so much for understanding!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 바쁜 순간에 전화가 온다면, 당황하지 말고 여러분의 상황을 정중하게 말해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if you get a call when you're busy, don't panic and try stating your situation politely.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "지금은 통화하기가 좀 어려워서요."
content.translation: It's a bit difficult to talk right now.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 업무 중에 개인적인 통화는 자제해 주세요. (Please refrain from personal calls during work.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be difficult / To be not possible
vocabulary.example: 지금은 제가 도움을 드리기가 조금 어렵네요. (It's a bit difficult for me to help you right now.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be awkward / To be in a tough spot
vocabulary.example: 갑자기 오시면 제가 좀 곤란해요. (It's a bit tough for me if you come suddenly.)
지금 바쁘니까 나중에 다시 이야기해요. (I'm busy now, so let's talk again later.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be sorted / To be finished
vocabulary.example: 회의가 정리되는 대로 바로 가겠습니다. (I'll go as soon as the meeting is finished.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be tactless / Not sensing the situation
vocabulary.example: 그는 눈치가 없어서 분위기를 자주 망쳐요. (He is tactless, so he often ruins the mood.)
